title: letrec/internal-define local functions stored in module-level globals — multiple closure-object instances alias each other's state
goal: Found while fixing ESH-0074 (PR #83). Internal-define (letrec*) local functions are emitted as @letrecstar_* MODULE-LEVEL globals; each activation of the enclosing function OVERWRITES those globals, and the returned dispatcher/closure captures the global's ADDRESS rather than a per-activation value. So two instances of a closure-based object share one backing — e.g. (define a1 (make-account 100)) (define a2 (make-account 50)) end up aliasing balance. Reproduces even with a let-bound (non-parameter) balance, so it is INDEPENDENT of the ESH-0074 set!/capture fix. Single-instance message-passing now works (PR #83); MULTI-instance does not.
repro: make-account with internal `(define (withdraw ...))/(define (deposit ...))/(define (dispatch m) ...)`; create two accounts; mutating one is visible in the other. The explicit let-lambda object form (no internal defines) works — confirming the bug is in letrec local-function codegen, not capture.
root_cause_hint: lib/backend/binding_codegen.cpp letrec/letrecStar codegen emits local functions as @letrecstar_* module globals (one storage shared across all activations). Needs per-activation allocation: each call to the enclosing function must get its own instances of the internal functions/closures, captured by value in the returned closure — not a module global address.
acceptance: two make-account instances have independent balances; mutating one does not affect the other; -r AND AOT
SICP ch3 multi-instance object patterns (accounts, make-monitored, etc.) isolate per instance
no regression to ESH-0074 (#83) single-instance, named-let (#50), TCO, or closure_edge tests
verification: PR #89 / 168aa74b: --no-stdlib recursive internal-define repro passes JIT + AOT
PR #89 / 168aa74b: tests/sicp/ch3_accounts.esk passes JIT + AOT with multi-instance account checks

PR #89 / 8fa8edbf: focused variadic/AD checks pass: append_variadic, redefine_variadic_to_fixed, closure_escape_branch, ad_input2, apply_variadic, map_variadic, autodiff_closure_stress, nested_ad_loop
PR #89 / 8fa8edbf: ICC readiness ready/100, completion oracle 15/15 PASS
status_note: MERGED #89 / 66133f972535d0b982e60cbf4873b7c70ec5d236. Fixes local letrec/letrec* closure instance aliasing by using per-activation storage for local letrec cells, keeping top-level/global letrec on module globals, routing local letrec calls through closure storage instead of raw _func aliases, and forwarding self-recursive local letrec calls through the current function when TCO is not active. Follow-up 285b4677 fixes CI regressions by preserving fixed arguments for variadic closure calls, keeping captured/CPS self-calls on the closure path, and teaching gradient's runtime closure fallback to load local closure cells. Follow-up 449dde8d fixes linux-arm64 stdlib.o `fixup value out of range` by bounding variadic closure dispatch fixed-param cases to the current call site's supplied arg count instead of emitting 17 x 33 cases everywhere. Follow-up 8fa8edbf fixes shadowable-op letrec redirection by preventing stale outer-function letrec storage loads inside generated lambdas, teaching capture analysis about redirected builtin ops such as `walk`, and keeping arena-promoted local letrec storage visible as a user shadow. Follow-up 3d113fec fixes the remaining Windows ARM XLA timeout by bounding closure dispatch IR per call site: capture cases 0..16 instead of 0..32 and non-variadic argument dispatch up to static call-site arity plus a small cushion. Verification: ICC ready/100 before/after, build eshkol-run, git diff --check, matmul_accuracy AOT compile reduced from 89.32s / 248M object to 18.39s / 53M object, aarch64-pc-windows-msvc compile-only matmul_accuracy passed in 26.18s, ./a.out passed, focused closure/variadic/TCO/stress tests passed, and CI 14/14 SUCCESS. Multi-instance make-account passes in shared mutable capture and SICP ch3 account tests.
campaign_payoff: Completes SICP ch3 object-oriented support (multiple independent stateful objects) — the canonical use of closures-as-objects. Pairs with ESH-0074 to fully fix make-account.
